title: The One Where Rachel Is Late

jokes.text: I'm coming soon to a theater near you. I'm in THX. I'm unsuitable for children!
jokes.setup: Joey showing off his movie poster
jokes.punchline: He treats technical specifications and ratings as personal accomplishments
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey misunderstanding what makes someone impressive. Perfect character integration - only Joey would brag about being 'unsuitable for children.'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, check it out. It's a one-sheet from my movie. Look at that — Dolby Digital, 5.1 surround sound. I did that.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You did the surround sound?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, I was in it. And it's rated PG-13 — I got it down from an R. Me. I convinced them.

jokes.text: I started that! / I thought I did!
jokes.setup: Joey claims he started Oscar buzz for his movie
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately disputes this with equal conviction
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective back-and-forth showing both characters' egos. Economy is excellent but concept is straightforward.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm telling you, I started the Oscar buzz for my movie!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That is a lie, and you know it.

jokes.text: I love taking limos when nobody died.
jokes.setup: Discussion of taking a limo to the premiere
jokes.punchline: Monica's oddly specific appreciation for celebratory vs funeral limos
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted dark observation with excellent economy. The specificity makes it memorable and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: A limo! Oh my God, we're taking a limo to the premiere!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I love limos. They're so fancy and sleek when you're going to something fun, but they're so dark and depressing when you're going to a funeral.

jokes.text: That's right. Still no baby.
jokes.setup: Rachel's emotional speech about missing being pregnant
jokes.punchline: Cut to her still very pregnant and miserable, contradicting her sentiment
jokes.explanation: Good comedic juxtaposition between scenes. The timing and contrast work well to undercut Rachel's previous sentiment.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I really miss being pregnant. It was such a magical time.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I felt so connected to my body, so alive.

jokes.text: You.
jokes.setup: Someone offers Rachel a more comfortable seat
jokes.punchline: Rachel's single-word, accusatory response to Ross
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - maximum hostility in one word. Shows the tension between Ross and pregnant Rachel effectively.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross?

jokes.text: Like you haven't done enough.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to be helpful
jokes.punchline: Rachel sarcastically blaming him for getting her pregnant
jokes.explanation: Straightforward sarcasm that establishes the dynamic. Functional but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, this is YOUR fault! You got me pregnant!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? How is this my fault?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You were trying to be helpful!

jokes.text: I wish I were a sea horse. Because with sea horses, it's the male. They carry the babies. And then also, um, I'd be far away in the sea.
jokes.setup: Ross wishing he could help with Rachel's discomfort
jokes.punchline: Rachel's elaborate seahorse fantasy that escalates from biology to escape
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist thinking with good escalation. The seahorse biology is unexpected, and the 'far away in the sea' addition elevates it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You know what? I wish you could help me. I really do. But unless you can turn into a seahorse, I don't think there's much you can do.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Why a seahorse?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Because the male seahorse is the one who gets pregnant. And I just think that would be fair.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I mean, wouldn't that be great? We could just switch off every nine months? You carry it for a while, then I carry it...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: —and then we could just give it to Phoebe. I mean, she's always saying she wants to have kids. She could just take it.

jokes.text: That's a great story. Tell it while you're getting me some iced tea.
jokes.setup: Monica starting to relate to Rachel's pregnancy discomfort
jokes.punchline: Rachel sarcastically dismissing her and demanding service
jokes.explanation: Good structure combining false politeness with a demand. Shows Rachel's pregnancy-induced entitlement effectively.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know exactly how you feel. My back is killing me, and I'm so bloated.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh please. You ate a pizza.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: A big pizza!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, I'm carrying a human being inside of me. The only thing you're carrying is leftovers. Now get me some ice cream.

jokes.text: Oh, God, get out! Get out, get out, get out!
jokes.setup: Rachel in bathroom discomfort
jokes.punchline: Her desperate, repeated demands for privacy
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy moment. Gets a reaction but relies on situation more than wit.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Get out! Get out! GET OUT!

jokes.text: Do you want me to sit on you? Because I'll do it.
jokes.setup: Joey asking if she had the baby yet
jokes.punchline: Rachel's aggressive threat using her pregnancy weight
jokes.explanation: Creative threat that uses Rachel's current state as a weapon. Great economy and character-specific humor.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Did you have the baby yet?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, but if you ask me one more time, I'm gonna use this pregnancy weight to sit on you.

jokes.text: I sold four on eBay. You'll be sitting next to HotGuy372.
jokes.setup: Joey expecting six premiere tickets
jokes.punchline: His agent sold most of them online and he's sitting next to a random eBay user
jokes.explanation: Perfect Estelle behavior - completely inappropriate and absurd. The specific username 'HotGuy372' adds to the humor.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Estelle, what do you mean you sold them online? I asked you for six premiere tickets!

dialogue.speaker: Estelle
dialogue.line: Joey, do you know how much these are worth on eBay? I made a killing!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: But those were for my friends! Now I'm sitting next to some random eBay user at my own premiere!

dialogue.speaker: Estelle
dialogue.line: That's not my problem, Joey. You want premium tickets, you pay premium prices.

jokes.text: What time do you want to pick me up?
jokes.setup: Joey upset about only having one ticket
jokes.punchline: Estelle assumes she's his plus-one
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Estelle's complete self-interest. The casual assumption is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I only have one ticket to this thing.

dialogue.speaker: Estelle
dialogue.line: That's okay, I'll be your plus-one.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, no - this is for someone special.

dialogue.speaker: Estelle
dialogue.line: I AM special, Joey.

jokes.text: I would still like to be acknowledged. Because I'm pregnant, I'm invisible?
jokes.setup: Rachel saying she doesn't want to go to the premiere
jokes.punchline: Still wanting to be considered despite declining
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing pregnant woman's need for validation even when declining. Relatable humor.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I don't want to go to the premiere.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: But I still want to be considered.

jokes.text: Definitely not invisible.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking if pregnancy makes her invisible
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry comment about her very obvious pregnant state
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deadpan delivery. Maximum impact with minimal words, though the joke is fairly obvious.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Does pregnancy make me invisible or something?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, no. I can see you just fine. It's just that you're REALLY hard to miss right now.

jokes.text: Well, then somebody's snoozing.
jokes.setup: Chandler saying Ross isn't snoozing, he's teaching
jokes.punchline: Joey stubbornly sticking to his 'you snooze you lose' logic
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's literal thinking and commitment to phrases. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You snooze, you lose, man. That's the rule.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: He's not snoozing, he's teaching!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I don't care what you call it. Eyes closed, you're out.

jokes.text: I haven't seen this dress. / Star in a movie.
jokes.setup: Monica describing her revealing sequin dress
jokes.punchline: Chandler's deadpan suggestion for how Joey should decide
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler moment cutting through Monica's manipulation with logic. Clean structure and delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Just tell Joey to flip a coin. Heads, he comes with you. Tails, he stays home and practices his acting.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's not helpful.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, but it's honest.

jokes.text: Phoebe. Phoebe. Phoebe.
jokes.setup: Monica asking Joey to listen to his heart
jokes.punchline: His heart literally says Phoebe's name three times
jokes.explanation: Creative literal interpretation of 'listen to your heart.' Perfect Joey logic and the repetition adds comedic weight.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You need to listen to your heart, Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, I'm listening.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's saying... Phoebe. Phoebe. Phoebe.

jokes.text: Phoebe,Phoebe,Phoebe.
jokes.setup: Joey's heart telling him to pick Phoebe
jokes.punchline: He repeats it again, showing his heart's consistency
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the heart gag. Less impact than the original but maintains the bit effectively.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: My heart's telling me to pick Phoebe.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: My heart's telling me to pick Phoebe.

jokes.text: So you're never actually going to pay me back?
jokes.setup: Joey saying the premiere will be his way of paying Chandler back
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry observation about the logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler logic pointing out the flaw in Joey's reasoning. Clean delivery and character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So by that logic, if you ever save my life, I owe you a Porsche.

jokes.text: I don't think you're comprehending just how slutty this dress is.
jokes.setup: Joey choosing Chandler over Monica's dress offer
jokes.punchline: Monica doubling down on her dress's appeal
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive nature shows through. The word choice 'comprehending' makes it funnier than just 'understanding.'

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's a great dress, Joey. You'd look amazing in it.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Thanks, but I'm gonna go with Chandler's suggestion.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, fine. But when you're out there looking like that, don't come crying to me. Actually, you know what? You will look good. You'll look so good that Chandler's gonna regret ever suggesting anything else.

jokes.text: Mon, maybe one of these guys wants to wear your dress.
jokes.setup: Monica upset about not being chosen
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's suggestion that the men could wear Monica's slutty dress
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe absurdist solution. Takes the dress conversation in an unexpected direction with her unique logic.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, the guys could wear your slutty dress.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's not a slutty dress!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh honey, I've seen you in that dress. It's a slutty dress.

jokes.text: I'm gonna go shave. Ahem. / Yeah, well, I'm gonna go, uh, spit.
jokes.setup: Emotional moment between Joey and Chandler about their friendship
jokes.punchline: Both men awkwardly fleeing the sentimental moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect male bonding awkwardness. Both characters fleeing genuine emotion with ridiculous excuses. Very character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I just want you to know that you mean a lot to me, man.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, you mean a lot to me too.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: We're like brothers.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We are brothers.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, this is getting too weird.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm gonna go.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, me too.

jokes.text: You hold her nose, I'll blow in her mouth and the kid'll just... Out of her.
jokes.setup: Discussion about when Rachel will have the baby
jokes.punchline: Chandler's ridiculous inflation method for birthing
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd visual. The casual delivery of something so ridiculous makes it funnier. Creative problem-solving gone wrong.

jokes.text: One hundred thousand dollars. / How about 50 bucks? / I'll call Zurich and move some money.
jokes.setup: Making a bet on when Rachel has the baby
jokes.punchline: Monica's absurd opening bet, Chandler's reality check, Monica's equally absurd response
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Monica's competitive nature. The Zurich line shows she's serious about her ridiculous bet.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I bet she has the baby on Christmas Day!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, that's like a one in 365 chance.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I'm feeling lucky!

jokes.text: Ahem, no one's here.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking whose turn it is to help her get up
jokes.punchline: She's alone and stuck
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy moment. Gets sympathy laughs but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Whose turn is it to help me get up?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh right, I'm alone and stuck.

jokes.text: A little tall. / What? / Would you crouch down a bit so that I look taller?
jokes.setup: Chandler asking how he looks at the premiere
jokes.punchline: Joey's vain request for Chandler to make him look better
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's vanity and self-centeredness even at his own premiere. Character-appropriate but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How do I look?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You look great!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, now make me look like that.

jokes.text: Yeah, like 50 bucks.
jokes.setup: Monica telling Rachel she looks great
jokes.punchline: Monica viewing Rachel as her winning bet rather than a friend
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's competitive nature overriding friendship. Good callback to the betting storyline with efficient delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You look great!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Really? Thank you!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm gonna win so much money on you.

jokes.text: I have to go pee. Apparently this baby thinks my bladder is a squeeze toy.
jokes.setup: Rachel needing to use the bathroom again
jokes.punchline: Vivid metaphor comparing baby's effect to a squeeze toy
jokes.explanation: Creative and relatable pregnancy observation. The squeeze toy metaphor is specific and visual.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I need to use the bathroom again. This baby is like a squeeze toy — every time I move, something comes out!

jokes.text: That's interesting that you lost. Now, I forget, do you like to lose?
jokes.setup: Monica losing the $50 bet
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's mock-innocent question about Monica's competitive nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe innocent-but-pointed question. She knows exactly how to needle Monica's competitive nature.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: So Monica, how much did you lose?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Fifty dollars.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wow. You know, I've always wondered... is it hard being so competitive?

jokes.text: Until then, General Grant, why don't you set up camp right there?
jokes.setup: Phoebe winning $50 from Monica
jokes.punchline: She addresses the $50 bill as General Grant and tells it to 'camp' in her area
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Phoebe wordplay. Addressing the bill by the person on it, using military camping metaphor for placing money. Creative, character-specific, and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Hello, General Grant. I want you to camp in my area.

jokes.text: People ask why we're not together. I just don't know what to tell them.
jokes.setup: Rachel being difficult and hostile toward Ross
jokes.punchline: Ross's deadpan comment about their relationship status
jokes.explanation: Good Ross self-aware moment. The dry delivery while dealing with pregnant Rachel's hostility works well.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So, we're not together, we're not broken up, we're in some kind of relationship limbo. I'm thinking of calling it 'The Rachel.'

jokes.text: Good God, man. Don't anger it.
jokes.setup: Ross commenting on Rachel's inappropriate outfit
jokes.punchline: Chandler treating pregnant Rachel like a dangerous creature
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler line treating Rachel like a wild animal. Perfect economy and memorable phrasing.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, that outfit is completely inappropriate for the office.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Nobody make any sudden movements. We don't want to startle her.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Just back away slowly and nobody gets hurt.

jokes.text: Uh-uh, pal! Don't call me 'Mommy.' It's bad enough you call your own mother that.
jokes.setup: Ross calling Rachel 'mommy'
jokes.punchline: Rachel's irritation expanding to criticize Ross's relationship with his own mother
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Rachel's pregnancy irritability extending beyond the immediate situation to general Ross criticism.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Did you seriously just call me 'mommy'?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? It just came out!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It just came out? Ross, that's not okay. And honestly, it explains so much about you and your actual mother. No wonder she still does your laundry.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: She does not—okay, she does, but that's different!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Is it though? Because right now you're looking at me the same way you look at her when she brings you soup.

jokes.text: I'm actually with her on this one.
jokes.setup: Rachel criticizing Ross for calling his mother 'Mommy'
jokes.punchline: Chandler agreeing that it's weird for a grown man
jokes.explanation: Simple Chandler aside. Works as a character moment but not particularly memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, that is weird. A grown man calling his mother 'Mommy.' I mean, at what point do you have to grow up and start calling her 'Ma' like the rest of us?

jokes.text: When Carol was pregnant with Ben... were you this irritating?
jokes.setup: Rachel and Ross in the doctor's office
jokes.punchline: Rachel directly asking if Ross was always this annoying during pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Direct and honest question that gets to the heart of their dynamic. Functional but not especially clever.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Were you always this annoying during pregnancy?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I wasn't pregnant, Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, that explains it.

jokes.text: Well, then, you must have a natural talent for it.
jokes.setup: Ross saying he's not trying to irritate her
jokes.punchline: Rachel's backhanded compliment about his irritating abilities
jokes.explanation: Well-crafted sarcastic response. The 'natural talent' phrasing elevates it from a simple insult.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm not trying to irritate you.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, you're doing a great job.

jokes.text: Seriously. Breathe louder, Ross. That's great.
jokes.setup: Uncomfortable silence between Rachel and Ross
jokes.punchline: Rachel finding even Ross's breathing annoying
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Rachel's irritation to the most basic level. Shows pregnancy hormones affecting everything.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, could you breathe any louder?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm just breathing normally.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, it's really annoying.

jokes.text: We should ask the doctor if she even knows... how to deliver a baby that's half human, half pure evil!
jokes.setup: Rachel's continued hostility toward Ross
jokes.punchline: She characterizes their unborn baby as half evil
jokes.explanation: Creative escalation of the Ross-Rachel tension. Calling their baby 'half pure evil' is both horrible and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I mean, come on, it's half you. It's going to be evil.

jokes.text: You're nice to her. She has the drugs.
jokes.setup: Ross being polite to the doctor
jokes.punchline: Rachel's practical explanation for being nice to medical staff
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about hospital politics. Rachel's practical wisdom about pain management access.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, you have to be nice to the doctor. I mean, he's got a needle.

jokes.text: You've got to be kidding me.
jokes.setup: Doctor suggesting sex to induce labor
jokes.punchline: Rachel's horrified reaction to having sex with Ross
jokes.explanation: Basic reaction to an awkward situation. Gets the job done but is fairly predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: You could try having sex. It might induce labor.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: With who?!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm right here.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I know!

jokes.text: I was surprised to see a kangaroo in a World War I epic.
jokes.setup: Joey asking about a specific scene in his war movie
jokes.punchline: Chandler's nonsensical response revealing he fell asleep
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist line. The historical incongruity of a kangaroo in WWI is perfect. Creative way to show he wasn't paying attention.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So in that scene where I'm storming the beach, did you see the part where I take out the machine gun nest?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, that was great. And then the giraffe showed up and you two danced the tango.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Chandler, were you asleep?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No! ...Okay, yes.

jokes.text: There was no kangaroo! They didn't take any of my suggestions!
jokes.setup: Chandler's kangaroo comment
jokes.punchline: Joey revealing he actually suggested a kangaroo for the WWI movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment. The idea that he actually suggested a kangaroo for WWI is perfectly in character and absurd. Great callback structure.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. A kangaroo? That was my idea!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, it wasn't.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yes, it was! I suggested it for that World War I movie!

jokes.text: But someone thinks that that will open up a can of worms.
jokes.setup: Discussion of sex to induce labor
jokes.punchline: Ross's euphemistic concern about relationship complications
jokes.explanation: Ross being cautious about their relationship complications. Character-appropriate but not particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I just don't want you to feel pressured, you know? I mean, if you're not comfortable with it...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, I'm nine months pregnant. I think I can handle it.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know, but... what if it's weird? What if things are... different?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Ross, just tell her you're worried about the baby.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, yes, but also... I don't want to see the baby!

jokes.text: Do it!
jokes.setup: Learning the one remaining method is sex
jokes.punchline: Monica's immediate enthusiastic endorsement
jokes.explanation: Monica's eagerness for the baby to be born. Simple but effective character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, what are we waiting for?

jokes.text: It's been a really long time for you. I mean, women have needs. Do it. Get yours.
jokes.setup: Monica encouraging Rachel to have sex with Ross
jokes.punchline: Monica's overly enthusiastic support becomes inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Monica's enthusiasm crossing into TMI territory. The 'get yours' phrasing makes it more awkward and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You should totally do it! I mean, Ross is a great guy, and you two have so much chemistry. Just go for it!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, I don't know...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, seriously! He's smart, he's funny, he's got that whole paleontologist thing going on. You'd be so lucky to sleep with him! I mean, I've seen him naked and—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, that's weird.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? I'm just saying, go jump his bones! Do it tonight! In fact, I could probably get Chandler out of the apartment if you want to use my room!

jokes.text: If Ross isn't willing, he's not the only guy you can have sex with. You could borrow Chandler. Chandler is good.
jokes.setup: Monica wanting Rachel to have sex to induce labor
jokes.punchline: Monica offering to lend out her boyfriend as an alternative
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation. Monica treating her boyfriend like a shared resource, plus the casual 'Chandler is good' endorsement.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know what? If you need to have sex to induce labor, I could... I could lend you Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What?!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, he owes me a favor!

jokes.text: While she's been going through this hell, you've been making money?
jokes.setup: Learning about the betting on Rachel's pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Ross's moral indignation about profiting from Rachel's misery
jokes.explanation: Ross being the moral center, pointing out the callousness of betting on Rachel's suffering. Functional character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You're betting money on Rachel's pregnancy? How could you do that? That's taking advantage of someone's misery for profit!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Says the guy who bet on whether Rachel would name the baby Emma or not.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's different!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How is that different?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I... I knew her!

jokes.text: I'm miserable. I may as well make some money out of it.
jokes.setup: Everyone feeling guilty about betting on her pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Rachel deciding to capitalize on her own misery
jokes.explanation: Good Rachel logic - if everyone else is profiting from her pain, she should too. Practical and character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, you guys were betting on whether I'm pregnant? That's... actually kind of genius. So what are the odds? Because I want in.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, no—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, I'm serious! If you're all going to profit off my potential misery, I should at least get a cut. What's my payout if I'm not pregnant?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's not really how it works...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, it should. Honestly, this is the most attention my uterus has gotten in years. I'm not letting it go to waste.

jokes.text: I don't want your... / Ah, ah, ah. We're doing this!
jokes.setup: Chandler saying Joey doesn't owe him anything
jokes.punchline: Joey's forceful insistence on repaying his debt
jokes.explanation: Joey's stubborn determination to clear his conscience. The interruption and insistence show his commitment to the gesture.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, you don't owe me anything.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, no! I'm gonna pay you back, Chandler. Every penny.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm serious. I always pay my debts.

jokes.text: Then there was the second set. The infamous 'booger' headshots.
jokes.setup: Joey calculating what he owes Chandler
jokes.punchline: Chandler referencing Joey's second set of headshots with a booger problem
jokes.explanation: Funny backstory detail. The word 'infamous' treats Joey's booger headshots like a historical event. Good character history.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So I owe you... $1,200?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, you could always do what you did with those headshots I paid for.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know, tell me you'll pay me back, then have a booger on your face the whole time.

jokes.text: Which we're still keeping under our hats.
jokes.setup: Mentioning Joey's tap dance classes
jokes.punchline: Chandler's aside about keeping Joey's tap dancing secret
jokes.explanation: Nice aside suggesting Joey's embarrassment about tap dancing. Adds character depth with humor.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We should probably keep Joey's tap dancing a secret. If word gets out that he's taking lessons, his tough guy image is done for.

jokes.text: Which, after 20 hours of lessons, still came out Jamaican. / The South will rise again, man.
jokes.setup: Chandler describing Joey's failed Southern accent lessons
jokes.punchline: Joey demonstrating his Jamaican-Southern accent hybrid
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup and payoff. The idea of confusing Southern and Jamaican accents is perfectly Joey. The line itself is incredibly quotable and shows his acting limitations.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So you're telling me you paid three hundred dollars for accent lessons?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, and it's working! Listen to this.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Howdy, mon. I'm fixin' to get some jerk chicken, ya heard?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's a Jamaican-Southern hybrid.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's a work in progress!

jokes.text: How do you clear this thing?
jokes.setup: Joey using a calculator to figure out what he owes
jokes.punchline: He's confused by basic calculator operation
jokes.explanation: Basic Joey stupidity moment. Very character-appropriate but not particularly creative.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, so I owe Chandler fifty bucks, right? Plus the twenty I borrowed last week... that's... seventy?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, no. Fifty plus twenty is... *presses calculator buttons repeatedly* ...how is it doing that?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It keeps saying seventy! Why would it say that?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because fifty plus twenty equals seventy, Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No it doesn't! It's fifty and twenty, not... *counts on fingers* ...Chandler, your calculator is broken.

jokes.text: By the way, you don't want to touch the pepper and then touch your eye.
jokes.setup: Ross warning about handling hot peppers
jokes.punchline: The timing suggests he's learned this from experience, likely just now
jokes.explanation: Classic warning-after-the-fact humor. The timing implies Ross just made this mistake himself.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, no! Don't touch your eyes! I'm serious, those peppers are really hot.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I learned that the hard way about five minutes ago.

jokes.text: Speaking of hot, watching you do that... really makes me want to have sex with you.
jokes.setup: Rachel eating hot peppers
jokes.punchline: Ross's inappropriate and poorly timed sexual comment
jokes.explanation: Ross's awkward attempt at seduction. The timing and delivery make it appropriately cringey.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's hot.

jokes.text: Make love'? What are you, a girl?
jokes.setup: Ross using the phrase 'make love'
jokes.punchline: Rachel mocking his romantic terminology
jokes.explanation: Rachel's practical approach to their situation, mocking Ross's romantic language. Good economy and character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Make love? Ross, what are you, a 1970s folk singer?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? It's a perfectly acceptable term for—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's creepy. Just say 'have sex' like a normal person.

jokes.text: Always a great way to get into a man's pants.
jokes.setup: Rachel calling him feminine
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically noting this isn't effective seduction
jokes.explanation: Ross's dry observation about seduction techniques. The role reversal of 'man's pants' adds to the humor.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, so calling me feminine isn't an effective seduction technique?

jokes.text: Think of me as a ketchup bottle. Sometimes, you have to bang on the end of it to get it to come out.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to convince Ross to have sex
jokes.punchline: Her comparing herself to a ketchup bottle needing to be hit
jokes.explanation: Hilariously unsexy metaphor. The visual comparison is both specific and absurd. Highly quotable and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Come on, Ross. Just hit me.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm like a ketchup bottle. Sometimes you just have to hit me to get what you want out.

jokes.text: I love when you talk dirty to me.
jokes.setup: Rachel's ketchup bottle analogy
jokes.punchline: Ross sarcastically calling it 'dirty talk'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross sarcasm responding to Rachel's unsexy metaphor. Good callback structure with efficient delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: It's like a ketchup bottle. You have to bang it on the bottom to get it to come.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's the dirtiest thing I've ever heard.

jokes.text: Oh, I seem to have dropped my fork. Let me just bend over and get it.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to seduce Ross
jokes.punchline: Her obvious attempt at a sexy move, hampered by pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Classic seduction attempt gone wrong due to pregnancy. The visual of a very pregnant woman trying to be sexy is inherently funny.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Hey Ross, don't you think I look... sexy?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I was going to do this sultry walk, but I can't really see my feet anymore.

jokes.text: Come on, wuss. Make love to me.
jokes.setup: Rachel's continued attempts to convince Ross
jokes.punchline: She combines name-calling with romantic language
jokes.explanation: Rachel's frustration leading to contradictory language - insulting while asking for romance. Good callback to her earlier mockery.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You're such an idiot, and I love you.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's not really how compliments work.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, it's how I work, you moron.

jokes.text: You'd think the jalapeño would clear up your sinuses, but no!
jokes.setup: Ross dealing with effects of spicy food
jokes.punchline: His false expectation about jalapeño benefits
jokes.explanation: Basic observational humor about spicy food expectations vs reality. Functional but not particularly memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I thought jalapeños were supposed to be good for you!

jokes.text: I think my water just broke. / I am good!
jokes.setup: After Ross's vigorous efforts to induce labor
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately taking credit for the timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross ego moment. His immediate self-congratulation for the timing is classically Ross. Good payoff to the labor induction subplot.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I did it! I did it! The baby's coming!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, the doctor said it could happen any time now.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, but it happened NOW. After I did all those things!

jokes.text: How did you know that? / You know everything! / Wait. I bet you the baby is over 7 pounds.
jokes.setup: Monica impressed by Phoebe's knowledge about Rachel
jokes.punchline: Monica immediately wanting to bet on Phoebe's insider information
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica competitiveness - immediately wanting to exploit Phoebe's apparent psychic abilities for betting advantage.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wow, you really know a lot about Rachel!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, I pick up on things.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm putting fifty bucks on whatever you say next.

jokes.text: I bet you it has hair! I bet you it's a girl. / We know it's a girl. / I'll give you really good odds.
jokes.setup: The betting continuing even as Rachel goes into labor
jokes.punchline: Phoebe trying to bet on something they already know
jokes.explanation: Shows how the betting has become compulsive. Phoebe's willingness to offer good odds on a known fact is silly but in character.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'll bet twenty bucks the baby's a boy!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, we already know it's a girl.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, then I'll bet twenty bucks it's NOT a boy!

tmdb_overview: Monica and Phoebe bet on when Rachel's baby will be born as the due date comes and goes as Rachel becomes extremely irritable. Meanwhile, Joey invites Chandler to his big movie premiere and he manages to fall asleep during it. Also, Ross attempts to help Rachel speed things along by trying every possible method suggested by the doctor, except one.
insight: Rachel's labor crisis sustains tension across 65 jokes, but emotional weight outpaces comedic payoff.
